birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: Breeds in Mongolia, eastern Russia and Manchuria; migrates through eastern China and South-East Asia to Indonesia and northern Australia

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: It lives in Manchuria and extreme Southeast Siberia, breeding in the north and wintering in the south

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: It is native to Manchuria

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The eastern crowned warbler breeds in eastern Siberia from the Argun River eastwards and southwards into western Manchuria and into central Sichuan, the Korean Peninsula and Japan

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: thumb|left|250px|In Hong Kong It is found in northern Mongolia, Manchuria and southern Siberia

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: thumb|left|Two Japanese waxwings feeding on berries The Japanese waxwing breeds in the dense coniferous forest in the Russian Far East (the Amur basin, northern Primorsky Krai, and Sakhalin) and northeasternmost China (Manchuria, Heilongjiang province)

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: thumb|left|Flock in the species' typical reed bed habitat during winter in Finland, the northernmost part of its distribution thumb|left|Juvenile male perched and moving around in reed beds in the Netherlands The bearded reedling is native to temperate Europe and Asia, ranging from Spain, France and the British Isles to the Manchurian region, but its distribution tends to be quite spotty because of its habitat preference. Maps often show most of its Asian range as a single large continuous section (instead of more spotty), but this is due to limited details in monitoring data from this area relative to the western part of its range.{{cite web| last=Malzer | first=I

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: It breeds in Manchuria, eastern China and northern Korea ; it winters throughout Southeast Asia

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: In summer the Japanese bush warbler can also be found in Hokkaidō, Manchuria, Korea, and central China

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The Oriental pratincole is native to Asia, breeding from North Pakistan and the Kashmir region, and sporadically southwards towards the Maldives and Sri Lanka, Indochina, eastern China, Manchuria and the Philippines

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: It breeds in the Russian Far East, Manchuria and far northeastern Korea, where its natural habitats are temperate shrubland and grassland

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: It is found in Manchuria; it winters in Southeast Asia

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: It breeds in Manchuria, Korea and Japan; it winters in South-east Asia

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: It is native and breeds in Manchuria, the Russian Far East and neighboring areas; it winters in Indochina and southern China. The birds are occasionally seen in Japan. They have also been observed in Hong Kong and Singapore. The bird's population is unknown, but is not increasing or declining. The white-throated rock thrush is said to be rare in North Korea. However, it is relatively common in China, except on the coast. The bird's IUCN Red List category is "least concern". The white-throated rock thrush inhabits forested areas, shrubland and rocky areas

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: It breeds in eastern Manchuria and the Russian Far East and winters in central and southern China

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: It breeds in Mongolia and the East Palearctic; it winters in Manchuria, Korea, Sakhalin and Japan

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: It is migratory, breeding in Manchuria and migrating to central China, the province of Yunnan and northern Southeast Asia in the winter

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: It is found in Manchuria, Korea and Japan

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: It was known only in two locations in Manchuria and southeastern Siberia, separated by more than 1000 km; however, in 2018, a new breeding population was found in the Amur region, situated between the two

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The steppe eagle also breeds in large stretches of western and northern China such as Tian Shan, Xinjiang, the Gobi area, Gansu, Ningxia, northern Tibet (by far their southernmost breeding area), Inner Mongolia and reach their eastern breeding limits in Manchuria and elsewhere in northeastern China

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: It breeds in East Siberia to North Manchuria wintering to West China, Myanmar, and Northeast India

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: |publisher=birds.kz |access-date=2013-04-23}} * Aquila chrysaetos daphanea Severtzov, 1888 – known variously as the Asian golden eagle, Himalayan golden eagle or berkut. This subspecies is distributed in central Kazakhstan, eastern Iran, and the easternmost Caucasus, distributed to Manchuria and central China and along the Himalayas from northern Pakistan to Bhutan and discontinuing in northeastern Myanmar (rarely ranging over into northernmost India).{{cite book|author1=Rasmussen, PC |author2=Anderton, JC| year=2005| title=Birds of South Asia

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: It is found in Manchuria and eastern China and the Russian Far East

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: It is found in Manchuria, southeastern Russia, northeastern Mongolia, central China and Korea
